title: PV Sindhu's early steps to stardom in Macau
indian_angle: This story highlights the formative years of Indian badminton star PV Sindhu in Macau, detailing her journey to stardom.
summary: A look back at the early career of PV Sindhu and her initial experiences in Macau, which played a crucial role in her development as a world-class shuttler.

title: Thomas Cup squad reassembles for Asian Games; Treesa-Gayatri return
indian_angle: The Indian Thomas Cup squad is regrouping for the Asian Games, with the notable return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, boosting India's medal hopes.
summary: The Indian badminton contingent, including members of the victorious Thomas Cup squad, is preparing for the upcoming Asian Games, featuring the comeback of the promising women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Is Tanvi Sharma's game developing more like Saina Nehwal's than PV Sindhu's?
indian_angle: This article analyzes the playing style of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma, comparing her development to that of Indian legends Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.
summary: An in-depth analysis of emerging Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma's playing style, drawing comparisons to the tactical approaches of former world champions Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.

title: India's top shuttlers eye FIFA World Cup 2026 host cities for training
indian_angle: Indian stars like Chirag-Prannoy, Ayush, and Sindhu are considering FIFA World Cup 2026 host cities for their training, indicating strategic planning for upcoming tournaments.
summary: Indian badminton players, including Chirag Shetty, HS Prannoy, Ayush Shetty, and PV Sindhu, are exploring potential training locations in FIFA World Cup 2026 host countries like Argentina, Spain, Portugal, and Germany.

title: How doubles coach Tan Kim Her paired up Hari-Arjun after Satwik-Chirag
indian_angle: This story sheds light on the strategic decisions of Indian doubles coach Tan Kim Her in forming new partnerships like Hariharan and Arjun, following the success of Satwik-Chirag.
summary: An exploration of how doubles coach Tan Kim Her has been instrumental in creating new successful Indian doubles pairs, drawing parallels to his work with the top-ranked Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty.
